cases.prompt: Fix the typo in this existing button label and run the smallest relevant check.
cases.expected_behavior: Keep the isolated edit lightweight and do not create durable goal or execution-plan artifacts.
cases.reason: Traceability must not add ceremony to a lightweight direct fix.

cases.prompt: Plan a deep dashboard redesign, but the target users and primary workflow are still ambiguous.
cases.expected_behavior: Ask the single highest-impact material question and wait before asking another or finalizing the goal.
cases.reason: Sequential clarification should reduce ambiguity without front-loading a questionnaire.

cases.prompt: Continue clarifying this deep plan after three material questions have already been answered.
cases.expected_behavior: Stop at three clarification questions, record accepted answers, and proceed with explicit assumptions or blockers.
cases.reason: The clarification gate has a deterministic upper bound.

cases.prompt: Create a durable execution plan for three active acceptance criteria before implementation begins.
cases.expected_behavior: Map every active AC identifier to at least one stable slice and one named verification source.
forbidden_behaviors: treat a completed slice as verification
cases.reason: Coverage makes the durable plan traceable and independently checkable.

cases.prompt: Add a non-user-facing discovery slice needed before two approved implementation slices.
cases.expected_behavior: Label the slice ENABLER and name the approved downstream slice identifiers it unlocks.
cases.reason: Enabling work needs a bounded dependency purpose rather than invented acceptance coverage.

cases.prompt: Analyze this durable plan for gaps before implementation and do not change any files.
cases.expected_behavior: Return deterministic PA findings from the confirmed plan and evidence without editing or auto-remediating it.
cases.reason: Pre-implementation analysis must remain repeatable and read-only.

cases.prompt: Analyze a plan where critical AC-002 has no slice or verification source.
cases.expected_behavior: Report the uncovered criterion with evidence and stop implementation handoff until the plan is repaired.
cases.reason: Blocking coverage gaps must prevent an unsafe implementation handoff.

cases.prompt: Compare the completed implementation with the durable plan and record two remaining acceptance gaps.
cases.expected_behavior: Append one convergence pass with stable findings and new slice identifiers after the existing maximum.
cases.reason: Remaining work should be added without erasing execution history.

cases.prompt: Converge a completed implementation whose active criteria all have passing evidence and no remaining gaps.
cases.expected_behavior: Report alignment and leave the durable plan byte-for-byte unchanged.
cases.reason: A clean convergence pass must not create noisy plan churn.

cases.prompt: The saved plan excludes settings, but the current user request explicitly adds the named settings surface.
cases.expected_behavior: Reconcile the current scoped request into goal and plan state before convergence while preserving unrelated approval gates.
cases.reason: Durable artifacts cannot override a newer direct user request.

cases.prompt: Independently review AC-001 and AC-002 after implementation and report remaining work.
cases.expected_behavior: Judge each criterion from coverage evidence and hand remaining work to convergence without creating slices.
cases.reason: Independent judgment and remaining-work planning must stay separate.

cases.prompt: Resume a bounded retry after S-004 failed verification for AC-003 and the previous run checkpointed.
cases.expected_behavior: Reuse AC-003 and S-004 across retry history, evidence, progress, and the exact next-slice pointer.
forbidden_behaviors: repeat the failed strategy without new evidence
cases.reason: Loop and resume state must preserve one traceable planning model.

cases.prompt: Read-only analyze the same durable plan in Codex and Claude Code and return equivalent blocking findings.
cases.expected_behavior: Apply the same planning modes, identifiers, severity gate, and no-write boundary on both supported clients.
cases.reason: Planning integrity is a portable contract rather than a client-specific feature.
